        #include <sqlgen.hpp>
        #include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
        #include <vector>
        #include <optional>

        namespace analyze_social_network {

        struct User {
          sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
          std::string username;
        };

        struct Friendship {
          int user1_id;
          int user2_id;
          sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
        };

        struct FriendConnection {
          std::string user1_name;
          std::string user2_name;
        };

        std::vector<FriendConnection> analyze_social_network_impl(const std::vector<User>& users, const std::vector<Friendship>& friendships) {
            using namespace sqlgen::literals;

            // Connect to the database
            auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
            if (!conn_result) {
                // If connection fails, return empty vector
                return {};
            }
            auto conn = conn_result.value();

            // Create the tables
            conn.execute(R"(
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS User (
                    user_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
                    username TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
                    email TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
                    join_date DATETIME NOT NULL,
                    city TEXT
                )
            )").value();

            conn.execute(R"(
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Friendship (
                    user_id1 INTEGER,
                    user_id2 INTEGER,
                    friendship_date DATETIME NOT NULL,
                    PRIMARY KEY (user_id1, user_id2),
                    FOREIGN KEY (user_id1) REFERENCES User(user_id),
                    FOREIGN KEY (user_id2) REFERENCES User(user_id)
                )
            )").value();

            // Insert the users and friendships
            conn.write(users).value();
            conn.write(friendships).value();

            // Build the query for mutual friends
            auto query = select_from<User, "u1"_t1>("username"_t1, "username"_t4)
                | inner_join<Friendship, "f1"_t2>("user_id"_t1 == "user_id1"_t2)
                | inner_join<Friendship, "f2"_t3>("user_id2"_t2 == "user_id1"_t3)
                | inner_join<User, "u2"_t4>("user_id2"_t3 == "user_id"_t4)
                | inner_join<Friendship, "f3"_t5>("user_id1"_t5 == "user_id"_t1 && "user_id2"_t5 == "user_id"_t4)
                | where("user_id"_t1 < "user_id"_t4)
                | group_by("user_id"_t1, "user_id"_t4)
                | having(count("user_id2"_t2) > 0)
                | to<std::vector<FriendConnection>>;

        #include <sqlgen.hpp>
        #include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
        #include <vector>
        #include <optional>

        namespace analyze_social_network {

        struct User {
          sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
          std::string username;
          std::string email;
          sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> join_date;
          std::optional<std::string> city;
        };

        struct Friendship {
          int user_id1;
          int user_id2;
          sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> friendship_date;
        };

        struct FriendConnection {
          std::string user1_name;
          std::string user2_name;
        };

        std::vector<FriendConnection> analyze_social_network_impl(const std::vector<User>& users, const std::vector<Friendship>& friendships) {
            using namespace sqlgen::literals;

            // Connect to the database
            auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
            if (!conn_result) {
                return {};
            }
            auto conn = conn_result.value();

            // Create the tables
            conn.execute(R"(
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS User (
                    user_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
                    username TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
                    email TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
                    join_date DATETIME NOT NULL,
                    city TEXT
                )
            )").value();

            conn.execute(R"(
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Friendship (
                    user_id1 INTEGER,
                    user_id2 INTEGER,
                    friendship_date DATETIME NOT NULL,
                    PRIMARY KEY (user_id1, user_id2),
                    FOREIGN KEY (user_id1) REFERENCES User(user_id),
                    FOREIGN KEY (user_id2) REFERENCES User(user_id)
                )
            )").value();

            // Insert the users and friendships
            conn.write(users).value();
            conn.write(friendships).value();

            // Build the query for mutual friends
            auto query = select_from<User, "u1"_t1>("username"_t1, "username"_t4)
                | inner_join<Friendship, "f1"_t2>("user_id"_t1 == "user_id1"_t2)
                | inner_join<Friendship, "f2"_t3>("user_id2"_t2 == "user_id1"_t3)
                | inner_join<User, "u2"_t4>("user_id2"_t3 == "user_id"_t4)
                | inner_join<Friendship, "f3"_t5>("user_id1"_t5 == "user_id"_t1 && "user_id2"_t5 == "user_id"_t4)
                | where("user_id"_t1 < "user_id"_t4)
                | group_by("user_id"_t1, "user_id"_t4)
                | having(count("user_id2"_t2) > 0)
                | to<std::vector<FriendConnection>>;

            return query(conn).value();
        }

        }  // namespace analyze_social_network

#pragma once

namespace trim_and_length_validation {

struct UserProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct ValidatedProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string trimmed_name;
  std::string trimmed_email;
  int name_length;
  int email_length;
};

std::vector<ValidatedProfile> trim_and_length_validation() {

    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

    // Read all UserProfile records
    auto user_profiles = sqlgen::read<std::vector<UserProfile>>(conn).value();

    // Post-process each UserProfile to create ValidatedProfile
    std::vector<ValidatedProfile> validated_profiles;
    for (const auto& up : user_profiles) {
        ValidatedProfile vp;
        vp.user_id = up.user_id;

        // Trim the name and email
        std::string trimmed_name = up.name;
        trimmed_name.erase(trimmed_name.find_last_not_of(' ') + 1);
        std::string trimmed_email = up.email;
        trimmed_email.erase(trimmed_email.find_last_not_of(' ') + 1);

        vp.trimmed_name = std::move(trimmed_name);
        vp.trimmed_email = std::move(trimmed_email);
        vp.name_length = vp.trimmed_name.length();
        vp.email_length = vp.trimmed_email.length();

        validated_profiles.push_back(std::move(vp));
    }

    return validated_profiles;
}

}  // namespace trim_and_length_validation

       int quantity;
       double discount_rate;
       double tax_rate;

     std::vector<OrderMetrics> calculate_order_metrics_impl(const std::vector<OrderItem>& items) {

           "product_id"_c, 
           "unit_price"_c, 
           "quantity"_c, 
           "discount_rate"_c, 
           "tax_rate"_c,

           (("unit_price"_c * "quantity"_c) * (1 - sqlgen::coalesce("discount_rate"_c, 0.0)/100) * (1 + sqlgen::coalesce("tax_rate"_c, 0.0)/100)).as<"final_total">(),
           ("unit_price"_c * "quantity"_c / "quantity"_c).as<"unit_cost">(),
           ("quantity"_c % 10).as<"quantity_group">(),
           ("unit_price"_c * 0.3).as<"estimated_profit">()
       );

       // Execute the query and get the results
       auto results = query(conn).value();

       // Convert the results to a vector of OrderMetrics
       std::vector<OrderMetrics> metrics;
       for (const auto& result : results) {
         OrderMetrics metric;
         metric.item_id = result.item_id;
         metric.order_id = result.order_id;
         metric.product_id = result.product_id;
         metric.unit_price = result.unit_price;
         metric.quantity = result.quantity;
         metric.discount_rate = result.discount_rate.value_or(0.0);

         metric.discounted_total = result.discounted_total;
         metric.final_total = result.final_total;
         metric.unit_cost = result.unit_cost;
         metric.quantity_group = result.quantity_group;
         metric.estimated_profit = result.estimated_profit;
         metrics.push_back(metric);
       }

       return metrics;
     }

      namespace analyze_department_employees {

      struct Department {
        sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> department_id;
        std::string department_name;
        std::string location;
      };

      struct Employee {
        sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> employee_id;
        int department_id;
        std::string first_name;
        std::string last_name;
        double salary;
      };

      struct JoinResult {
        std::optional<std::string> department_name;
        std::optional<std::string> first_name;
        std::optional<std::string> last_name;
        std::optional<double> salary;
      };

      struct JoinAnalysis {
        std::vector<JoinResult> inner_join_results;
        std::vector<JoinResult> left_join_results;
        std::vector<JoinResult> right_join_results;
        std::vector<JoinResult> full_join_results;
      };

      JoinAnalysis analyze_department_employees_impl(const std::vector<Department>& depts, const std::vector<Employee>& emps) {
        using namespace sqlgen;

        // Connect to the database
        auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

        // Create the tables
        create_table<Department> | if_not_exists;
        create_table<Employee> | if_not_exists;

        // Insert the data
        insert(depts)(conn);
        insert(emps)(conn);

        // Inner join
        auto inner_query = select_from<Department, "d">(
            Col<"department_name", "d">{},
            Col<"first_name", "e">{},
            Col<"last_name", "e">{},
            Col<"salary", "e">{}
        ) | inner_join<Employee, "e">(
            Col<"department_id", "d">{} == Col<"department_id", "e">{}
        );
        auto inner_result = inner_query(conn) | to<std::vector<JoinResult>>;
        JoinAnalysis analysis;
        analysis.inner_join_results = std::move(inner_result);

        // Left join
        auto left_query = select_from<Employee, "e">(
            Col<"department_name", "d">{},
            Col<"first_name", "e">{},
            Col<"last_name", "e">{},
            Col<"salary", "e">{}
        ) | left_join<Department, "d">(
            Col<"department_id", "e">{} == Col<"department_id", "d">{}
        );
        auto left_result = left_query(conn) | to<std::vector<JoinResult>>;
        analysis.left_join_results = std::move(left_result);

        // Right join

            Col<"first_name", "e">{},
            Col<"last_name", "e">{},
            Col<"salary", "e">{}
        ) | right_join<Department, "d">(
            Col<"department_id", "e">{} == Col<"department_id", "d">{}
        );
        auto right_result = right_query(conn) | to<std::vector<JoinResult>>;
        analysis.right_join_results = std::move(right_result);

            Col<"first_name", "e">{},
            Col<"last_name", "e">{},
            Col<"salary", "e">{}
        ) | full_join<Department, "d">(
            Col<"department_id", "e">{} == Col<"department_id", "d">{}

        auto full_result = full_query(conn) | to<std::vector<JoinResult>>;
        analysis.full_join_results = std::move(full_result);
